domain: Inuit kayak skin-on-frame (driftwood frame persistent year-on-year; seal-skin replaced each season; properties live in skin; safety lives in frame)
specific_mechanism: Two-persistence architecture: a slow-changing core (FRAME) carries identity/safety invariants; a fast-replaced outer layer (SKIN) carries session-specific properties; SKIN cannot override FRAME by architectural construction.
llm_application: SOF-LLM: split deployment architecture. FRAME = small persistent identity tensor + alignment-invariant subspace, enforced via PROJECTION constraints. SKIN = LoRA + exemplar buffer + KV header, REPLACED per session/user. SKIN updates are restricted to the orthogonal complement of FRAME's projection — architecturally cannot influence safety/persona.
novelty_claim: Activated LoRA (2512.17910) swaps adapters efficiently but no projection-based persistence-segregation; RECAST (2411.16870) blueprint+module is symmetric; layer-swap (2506.02006) is runtime not architectural. SOF-LLM's projection-enforced two-persistence-layer with SKIN constrained to FRAME's orthogonal complement is a stricter architectural property than published.
